name: Links
description: Three-in-one utility: buffered 1:3 multiple, precision 2:2 adder/multiple, and 3:1 averaging mixer. DC-coupled throughout, accepts audio rate, 0.1% resistors on the adder for note-CV transposition duties.

inputs.name: 1:3 IN
inputs.description: Input to the buffered multiple. Signal is duplicated with no voltage drop across three OUT jacks.
inputs.voltage: DC-coupled · audio rate OK · 1MΩ input impedance

inputs.name: 2:2 IN A
inputs.description: First input of the precision 2-in summer. Summed with IN B with unity gain on each.

inputs.name: 2:2 IN B
inputs.description: Second input of the precision 2-in summer. Leave unpatched to use the section as a 1:2 multiple of IN A.

inputs.name: 3:1 IN 1
inputs.description: First input of the 3-in averaging mixer. Signal contributes with gain of 1/3 to the sum.

inputs.name: 3:1 IN 2
inputs.description: Second input of the averaging mixer. Contributes 1/3 to the sum.

inputs.name: 3:1 IN 3
inputs.description: Third input of the averaging mixer. Contributes 1/3 to the sum.

outputs.name: 1:3 OUT (×3)
outputs.description: Three identical buffered copies of the 1:3 IN signal. Low offset error — suitable for splitting 1V/oct to multiple VCOs.

outputs.name: 2:2 OUT (×2)
outputs.description: Two identical copies of the sum of 2:2 IN A + IN B. 0.1% precision resistors preserve pitch accuracy.

outputs.name: 3:1 OUT
outputs.description: Single output carrying (IN 1 + IN 2 + IN 3) / 3. Averaging prevents clipping when mixing modular-level audio.

behaviors.name: Precision 1V/oct distribution
behaviors.description: The 1:3 buffered multiple is designed for splitting a pitch CV across multiple VCOs without tuning drift. Passive stackcables and unbuffered mults can slump at the third tap; Links preserves the voltage identically at all three outputs.

behaviors.name: Precision adder / transposer
behaviors.description: 0.1% resistors make the 2:2 section a precision summer: sequencer CV into IN A plus keyboard CV into IN B gives a transposed sequence at OUT. Unlike many mixers, the unity gains are exact — musical intervals stay musical.

behaviors.name: 1:2 mult via unused input
behaviors.description: When only IN A is patched, the 2:2 section becomes a buffered 1:2 multiple (two identical copies of IN A). Combined with the 1:3 section, one Links gives 5 buffered copies of a single note CV.

behaviors.name: Averaging prevents audio clipping
behaviors.description: The 3:1 section applies a gain of 1/3 per input. Three full-scale VCOs sum to full scale at the output — no soft-clip, no distortion, no attenuators needed upstream. The trade-off is 9.5dB less gain than a unity-gain mixer.

behaviors.name: DC-coupled across all sections
behaviors.trigger: modulation or pitch signals through any section
behaviors.description: Every section is DC-coupled. CVs, LFOs, envelopes, and audio all pass without filtering. The averager works equally well as a 3-source CV mixer (three modulations combined into one control voltage) as it does as an audio bus.

patchIdeas.name: Split pitch to three VCOs
patchIdeas.patch: Sequencer pitch CV → 1:3 IN · three OUTs → V/Oct of three VCOs for a detuned unison or stacked intervals · since 1:3 is buffered with 0.1% precision, all three stay in tune after hours without the usual passive-mult sag.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Links:

Sequencer:
VCO 1:
VCO 2:
VCO 3:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Links (1:3 IN)
- Links (1:3 OUT 1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Links (1:3 OUT 2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Links (1:3 OUT 3) p> VCO 3 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Keyboard transposes a sequence
patchIdeas.patch: Sequencer pitch → 2:2 IN A · keyboard CV → 2:2 IN B · 2:2 OUT → VCO V/Oct · the 2:2 adder sums them exactly, so playing a C on the keyboard shifts the running sequence up by a fifth to the ear · unplug keyboard to return to original pitch.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Links:

Sequencer:
Keyboard:
VCO:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Links (2:2 IN A)
- Keyboard (CV) p> Links (2:2 IN B)
- Links (2:2 OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Three-oscillator drone mix
patchIdeas.patch: Three VCOs tuned to root, fifth, octave · each into 3:1 IN 1/2/3 · 3:1 OUT → filter → output · averaging keeps the sum below clipping; raise output volume at the mixer downstream · ideal for drone voices built from raw oscillator outputs.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Links:

VCO 1:
VCO 2:
VCO 3:
Filter:

- VCO 1 (Saw) -> Links (3:1 IN 1)
- VCO 2 (Saw) -> Links (3:1 IN 2)
- VCO 3 (Saw) -> Links (3:1 IN 3)
- Links (3:1 OUT) -> Filter (In) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Modulation averaging
patchIdeas.patch: Three slow LFOs at different rates → 3:1 · output becomes a compound modulation that never repeats · 3:1 OUT → filter cutoff · the averaged CV stays centered (sum of three bipolar sources averages toward zero) unlike a unity summer which accumulates.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Links:

LFO 1:
LFO 2:
LFO 3:
Filter:

- LFO 1 (Out) >> Links (3:1 IN 1)
- LFO 2 (Out) >> Links (3:1 IN 2)
- LFO 3 (Out) >> Links (3:1 IN 3)
- Links (3:1 OUT) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Five-way buffered pitch mult
patchIdeas.patch: Sequencer → 1:3 IN · take three buffered copies · route Sequencer also into 2:2 IN A (leave IN B unpatched) · 2:2 gives two more precision copies · total five in-tune pitch CVs from a single Links · use for stacked VCO rig or sending the same pitch to multiple quantizers/shifters.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Links:

Sequencer:
VCO 1:
VCO 2:
VCO 3:
VCO 4:
VCO 5:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Links (1:3 IN)
- Links (1:3 OUT 1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Links (1:3 OUT 2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Links (1:3 OUT 3) p> VCO 3 (V/Oct)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Links (2:2 IN A) // IN B unpatched
- Links (2:2 OUT A) p> VCO 4 (V/Oct)
- Links (2:2 OUT B) p> VCO 5 (V/Oct)
